title: coleus bourneae
intro: Coleus bourneae, also known as the 'Red Backed Coleus', is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Coleus bourneae lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and exhibit antimicrobial activities, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Coleus bourneae can be made by infusing the leaves in water or using them as a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
